basics.name: Michael Becker
basics.label: Senior Software Engineer · Tech Lead · .NET / Backend Systems
basics.summary: Tech Lead and Senior Software Engineer with 20+ years of experience designing and delivering enterprise software for international clients across the Americas and Europe. Specialist in .NET, Backend Systems, and modern architectures (DDD, CQRS, Clean Architecture). Eager to build strong engineering teams and systems and help developers grow. Passionate about coding, automation, simplicity, and shipping incremental, high-quality value.

work.name: IntrosMatter
work.summary: Lead an 8-person engineering team — architecture, solution design, and engineering standards — while staying hands-on as a back-end contributor.
highlights: Lead an 8-person engineering team, owning architecture, solution design, and engineering standards across projects.
Designed new solutions end-to-end and stayed involved through implementation as a hands-on back-end contributor.
Defined team processes and development best practices to drive code quality and delivery consistency.
Maintained the project documentation wiki to support onboarding and knowledge sharing.
Partnered with DevOps to set up CI/CD pipelines; managed GitLab repositories and branching strategy.
AI-assisted development (Claude Code, Cursor, GitHub Copilot) — defining team-wide practices for effective use and embedding them into the development workflow.
work.details:
Joined IntrosMatter as a back-end contributor while still leading a BairesDev Partnerships team. The codebase had been built on BairesDev's internal legacy framework, and I led a full refactor toward Clean Architecture so we could decouple from the internal framework one module at a time — without ever slowing feature delivery. As the project hired and grew, I moved over full-time and stepped up as Tech Lead.

The team grew to eight engineers: three back-end (myself included), two full-stack, one front-end, and two QA. I also took on most of the project-manager responsibilities alongside the tech-lead role for about six months, until a dedicated PM joined for the final four months of the project.

The piece-by-piece transition let the new team choose technologies that genuinely fit the project as we replaced each framework dependency, and we landed on a fully independent, scalable platform. I also formalized and documented every team process — ticket workflow, code review, release and deploy, hotfix and restore procedures — giving the team a stable operating manual as we scaled.

work.name: BairesDev — Core Team
work.summary: Promoted to Technical Lead within four months. Architected a new backend architecture and an integrated automated-testing solution.
highlights: Promoted to Technical Lead within 4 months of joining the Core Team.
Drove DDD, CQRS, and Clean Architecture patterns across the Partnerships codebase.
Designed and implemented an integrated automated-testing solution for the team.
work.details:
Joined the BairesDev Core team to work on Partnerships, a small project staffed with one Tech Lead, two back-end engineers (me included), one front-end engineer, and one QA. The codebase was build from the ground using on BairesDev's internal framework, which presented several real challenges — the patterns I learned working through them later informed the decoupling work I led at IntrosMatter.

After four months I was promoted to Tech Lead of the team and stayed on the project until the codebase reached a stable, maintainable state. Partnerships is still cited internally at BairesDev Core by developers as one of the cleanest and easiest-to-maintain projects in the portfolio.

work.name: FoodX (via BairesDev)
work.summary: Built event-driven microservices on Azure and owned feature design and delivery with DDD and Clean Architecture standards.
highlights: Promoted to Team Lead within 4 months.
Built event-driven microservices on Azure (Functions, Service Bus, Storage Queues, non-relational storage).
Lead 3 core services and owned feature design and delivery for them.
Implemented projects with Infrastructure as Code and fully automated CI/CD pipelines.
Owned feature design and delivery with DDD, Clean Architecture and tests with 99% code coverage.
work.details:
My first long-term engagement after joining BairesDev. FoodX was a Vancouver startup serving a single anchor client — Carrefour Belgium — and together we designed, built, and went live with a full e-grocery platform: storage, order capture, fulfillment, and delivery all fully connected. I joined as a back-end developer and was promoted to Tech Lead within four months, owning three of the core services: Order Management, Smart Substitutions, and Intelligent Packaging.

Working alongside engineers who had come from Amazon and Microsoft, this was the project where I really developed my passion for back-end systems — event-driven architecture, message-bus patterns, and the operational realities of running production services at scale. The startup ultimately couldn't sustain itself past the two-year mark, but the technical depth I built there still shapes how I design systems today.
